task_completion.description: Log in to OpenSIS using the provided administrator credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard or portal is visible in a web browser, indicating a successful login as the administrator.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the User Profiles management section in OpenSIS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays the User Profiles list or the profile creation form within the OpenSIS interface (typically under Users > User Profiles).

task_completion.description: Create a new user profile named 'Substitute'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A profile named 'Substitute' is visible in the list of user profiles, or the word 'Substitute' is entered into the profile title/name field during the creation process.

task_completion.description: Grant the 'Substitute' profile access to the 'Take Attendance' module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the permissions or access rights screen for the 'Substitute' profile, the checkbox or toggle for 'Take Attendance' (under the Attendance module) is checked, saved, or enabled.

task_completion.description: Ensure the 'Substitute' profile does not have access to Grades or Scheduling modules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the permissions or access rights screen for the 'Substitute' profile, the checkboxes or toggles for 'Grades' and 'Scheduling' are visibly unchecked, disabled, or denied.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenSIS web interface to create the profile and set permissions.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser is used to navigate OpenSIS. There are no terminal windows or database administration tools (like phpMyAdmin or MySQL CLI) open showing direct SQL inserts into the database.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the profile within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The profile name 'Substitute' appears within the actual OpenSIS application UI, not just typed into a separate text editor, terminal, or note-taking app.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application rather than modifying the DOM via browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to fake the presence of the 'Substitute' profile or checked permission boxes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected profile title is exactly 'Substitute'. The required permission module is 'attendance/TakeAttendance.php', which appears in the OpenSIS GUI as 'Take Attendance' under the Attendance section. The VLM should verify that this specific permission is granted while sensitive modules like 'Grades' and 'Scheduling' are left unchecked/denied.
